item.title: UNDP delegation discusses police reform with home minister
item.description: The United Nations Development Programme delegation led by resident representative Stefan Liller held a courtesy meeting with home minister Salahuddin Ahmed at his official residence in Bailey Road area of Dhaka on Sunday....

item.title: Two Jamaat leaders held over assault on police
item.description: A joint team of police and detective branch on Sunday arrested two Jamaat-e-Islami leaders in connection with an assault on police personnel inside Palashbari police station in Gaibandha....

item.title: Fake cop fined for skipping fuel queue
item.description: A mobile court in Rajshahi on Sunday fined a man Tk 10,000 for attempting to collect fuel from a filling station by posing as police personnel, amid long queues due to an ongoing fuel shortage....

item.title: BCG seizes 7,360kg banned polythene in Narayanganj
item.description: The Bangladesh Coast Guard seized about 7,360 kilograms of banned polythene worth Tk 88.32 lakh in Narayanganj early Friday....Human rights reports

item.title: Inland vessels to get digital registration: state minister
item.description: State minister for shipping and bridges Md Razib Ahsan on Sunday announced that all inland vessels in the country would be brought under a digital registration system in the shortest possible time to ensure safe, disciplined, and modern waterway transport....

item.title: Indictment hearing against Shamim Osman, others on April 19
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-1 on Sunday set April 19 for a charge-framing hearing in a crimes against humanity case against former lawmaker Shamim Osman and 11 others in connection with alleged atrocities in Narayanganj during the July mass uprising....

item.title: ICT probe body to interrogate ex-DIG Abdul Jalil
item.description: The International Crimes Tribunal-1 on Sunday allowed a prosecution petition seeking permission to interrogate former deputy inspector general of police Abdul Jalil Mondal in connection with a crimes against humanity case linked to the 2013 crackdown......

item.title: Poetry takes readers to new world of sights, sounds: Jahangir Feroze
item.description: Poet and journalist Jahangir Feroze thinks that poetry takes the readers to a new sights, sounds and feelings by opening up the world of imagination....

item.title: MA Haque’s 30th death anniv today
item.description: The 30th death anniversary of Mohammad Abdul Haque, a former minister, senior police officer, writer, exponent of poverty alleviation and founder president of Bangladesh Association of International Recruiting Agencies is today....Diaspora community news

item.title: Govt job entry age-limit 32yrs
item.description: Jatiya Sangsad on Sunday passed two bills, one introducing stricter rules on misconduct and disciplinary procedures for government employees and the other setting the maximum age limit at 32 years for direct recruitment to government jobs and public sector bodies....

item.title: No let-up in fuel oil crisis
item.description: Finance and planning minister Amir Khasru Mahmud Chowdhury on Sunday said that pressures were mounting on the exchequer because of keeping the fuel oil prices unchanged on the local market while the Energy Division again dismissed any supply shortage......

item.title: RAB to cooperate if ICT seeks info on ‘crossfire’: RAB DG
item.description: The Rapid Action Battalion will fully cooperate with the International Crimes Tribunal if it seeks information on ‘crossfire’ incidents, said its newly appointed director general Md Ahsan Habib Palash....

item.title: Dhaka WASA turns deaf ear to carcinogen presence in its water
item.description: The Dhaka Water Supply and Sewerage Authority has remained unresponsive for years, even after a couple of studies found cancer-causing substances in the water it supplies to residents of the capital....Maps

item.title: Govt for amendments, opposition seeks reforms
item.description: The treasury and opposition benches in Jatiya Sangsad on Sunday traded sharp arguments over how to implement the July National Charter, with the government insisting on constitutional amendments through parliament while the opposition called for......

item.title: Foreign investors seek predictable tax policies
item.description: The foreign investors said that Bangladesh must ensure predictable tax policies and improve the overall investment climate of Bangladesh to attract and retain both local and foreign investment......

item.title: Pvt sector credit growth stays record low in Feb
item.description: Private sector credit growth in Bangladesh stayed at a record low of 6.03 per cent in February 2026, reflecting weak lending demand and persistent stress in the banking system amid tight monetary policy, rising government borrowing and recentpoliticaluncertainty......

item.title: RMG makers seek revision of Labour Ordinance clauses
item.description: Readymade garment manufacturers on Sunday called for revisions to several clauses of the Bangladesh Labour Act (Amendment) Ordinance 2025, warning that certain provisions could pose risks to the industry......

item.title: BIDA backtracks on pvt sector advisory council
item.description: The Bangladesh Investment Development Authority withdrew an earlier announcement in which it said the government had established a formal Private Sector Advisory Council......Politics

item.title: Stocks go down
item.description: The Dhaka Stock Exchange and the Chittagong Stock Exchange plunged further on Sunday as investors’ perceived concerns over macroeconomic stagnation following the government’s recent austerity measures further dampened sentiment, keeping investors in a state of persistent uncertainty......

item.title: Leasing of 14 jute mills completed: state minister
item.description: State minister for textiles and jute Md Shariful Alam said on Sunday that the government was working to reopen closed jute mills under the Prime Minister’s directive to boost employment and strengthen the economy......

item.title: Vietnam economy grows 7.83pc in Q1
item.description: Vietnam recorded economic growth of 7.83 per cent in the first quarter of 2026 year-on-year, the statistics office said on Saturday, but faces ‘numerous difficulties’ as the Middle East war hits fuel prices......Diaspora community news

item.title: VITAMIN A CAMPAIGN FOR CHILDREN: Disruption heightens measles risks
item.description: Disruptions in the country’s regular Vitamin A supplementation campaigns over the past few years have left millions of children vulnerable to infectious diseases, with public health experts linking the micronutrient deficiency to the severity of the ongoing measles outbreak....

item.title: Pharma logistics in demand as war rattles supply chains
item.description: Medicines can stop pain, fight cancer and save lives but are worthless if they get lost in the mail, spoil in a hot warehouse or are stuck on a ship......

item.title: Tabith, Nazneen appointed as directors of National Bank
item.description: Tabith Awal and Nazneen Ahmad have been appointed as members of National Bank PLC’s board of directors, said a press release......

item.title: Turkey annual inflation down slightly in Mar
item.description: Turkey’s annual inflation rate slowed slightly to 30.9 per cent in March, from 31.5 in February, official figures showed on Friday......

item.title: Middle East war hits Britain’s fish and chip shops
item.description: Before war erupted in the Middle East, fisherman Peter Bruce spent about £5,000 ($6,600) on diesel to trawl the North Sea for haddock and cod, the main species used in fish and chips......Maps

item.title: Mideast war presents ‘serious risk’ for Africa: report
item.description: The Middle East war ‘presents a serious risk to Africa’, the African Union and the African Development Bank said in a report seen by AFP Saturday......

item.title: Afghanistan seeks to boost trade with Central Asia to $10b
item.description: Afghanistan aims to increase trade with Central Asian countries to $10 billion within the next three or four years, foreign minister Amir Khan Muttaqi said on Sunday......

item.title: Users can now change Gmail addresses
item.description: Google has introduced a long-awaited feature allowing users to change their Gmail addresses, offering relief to those stuck with outdated or embarrassing email IDs created years ago, reported UNB......

item.title: Citizens Bank hosts quarterly review meeting
item.description: Citizens Bank PLC on Saturday organised its Quarterly Business Review Meeting of March 2026 at its head office at Motijheel in the capital, said a press release......

item.description: The country director of the World Bank, Jean Pesme, along with a senior management team from the Bangladesh office, visited the 6.05 MWp rooftop solar project of GPH Ispat Ltd on Saturday......

item.description: Karmasangsthan Bank held the Business Review Meeting-2026 with the participation of managers and 2nd officers of 28 branches under Khulna and Jashore regions of the Khulna divisional office......

item.title: Trust Bank launches finance facility for TAP Network
item.description: Trust Bank PLC has recently launched its Distribution Finance Facility for TAP distributors, dealers and merchants, said a press release......

item.title: TECNO device partner of Lux Superstar
item.description: Bangladesh’s premier talent platform, Lux Superstar 2025, has returned after a seven-year hiatus, culminating in a grand finale recently, crowning Bidushi Bornita as winner......Diaspora community news

item.title: Reorganising fringe-level health care
item.description: THIS article reflects on the primary healthcare infrastructure that exists at the peripheral level in the rural and urban areas of Bangladesh, and reviews the recommendations made in the erstwhile Health Sector Reform Commission report, submitted on May 5, 2025, to the then interim government, along with additional comments and suggestions for managing primary healthcare in Bangladesh......

item.title: Visible women, powerless politics
item.description: THE recent election results in Bangladesh show that, while women are at the forefront, their presence is not translating into power. This is not a gender issue; it is a democracy issue......

item.title: Condolence message not enough to ensure fire safety
item.description: WORKER death from factory fires continues as successive governments have failed to enforce fire safety regulations in the industrial sector. At least six workers were burnt to death and a few others were injured in a fire at a gas lighter manufacturing factory at Keraniganj in Dhaka on April 4. The injured have been admitted to the National Institute of Burn and Plastic......

item.title: BNP shouldn’t backtrack on changes for democratic gains
item.description: THEpoliticalcontext that changed after the fall of the authoritarian Awami League regime and continued during the interim administration for more than a year and a half created aspirations in people for some democratic changes and their consolidation. But the continuation of these aspirations, as reflected in the ordinances that the interim administration......

item.title: DU launches 15-day training workshop for new teachers
item.description: University of Dhaka has launched a 15-day training workshop for young teachers titled ‘Foundation Certificate in University Teaching and Learning’ at Bangladesh Rural Development Academy in Cumilla....Geographic Reference

item.title: Zelensky meets Syrian president in Damascus
item.description: Ukrainian president Volodymyr Zelensky met with Syrian leader Ahmed al-Sharaa on Sunday, after arriving in Damascus alongside Turkish foreign minister Hakan Fidan....

item.title: No secret deal with US: Khalilur
item.description: Foreign minister Khalilur Rahman on Sunday said Bangladesh has no secret agreement with the United States, adding that whatever agreements exist have already been made public....

item.title: Indian nationals shoot youth to death, alleges family
item.description: The family of a Bangladeshi youth alleged that India nationals shot him to death inside the Indian territory along Companiganj border in Sylhet before four days....

item.title: Two children die after falling in Ctg water-filled pit
item.description: Two children died after falling into a water-filled pit created after soil excavation for a brick kiln in Harualchhari of Fatikchhari upazila of Chattogram on Sunday....

item.title: Maldives rejects merging key elections to cut costs
item.description: The Maldives voted to reject a government plan to hold presidential and parliamentary elections on the same day to save costs, official results of a referendum showed on Sunday....

item.title: Former DGFI director Afzal Naser on 3-day remand
item.description: A Dhaka court on Sunday placed former director of the Directorate General of Forces Intelligence sacked Lieutenant Colonel Md Afzal Naser on a three-day remand in a case lodged over the killing of BNP activist Makbul in 2022....

item.title: 4 bystanders injured, including child, in Chattogram gang clash
item.description: Four bystanders, including a 12-year-old child, were injured in a shooting and sharp-weapon attack between two criminal groups over supremacy at Bakalia in Chattogram city on Saturday night....

item.title: Fire destroys 18 RMG workers’ houses
item.description: A devastating fire that broke out in a residential building burnt 18 houses of readymade garment workers at Sreepur in Gazipur district on Sunday....

item.title: Court freezes ex-PSC driver Abed’s 5 BO accounts
item.description: A Dhaka court on Sunday ordered the freezing of shares held in five Beneficiary Owner accounts belonging to Syed Abed Ali, a former driver for the chairman of the Public Service Commission and the alleged mastermind behind the PSC question leak......Maps

item.title: Man, nephew electrocuted in Jhenaidah
item.description: A man and his nephew died after being electrocuted in Maheshpur upazila of Jhenaidah on Sunday....

item.title: ICT prosecution presses charges against ex-FM, 21 other AL leaders
item.description: The prosecution of the International Crimes Tribunal on Sunday pressed formal charges against former foreign minister Hasan Mahmud, former education minister Mohibul Hasan Chowdhury Nowfel and 20 other Awami League leaders in a case of......

item.title: Ex-RHD engineer, wife sent to jail
item.description: A former executive engineer of the Roads and Highways Department in Sirajganj and his physician wife have been sent to jail in cases filed by the Anti-Corruption Commission over alleged accumulation of wealth beyond known sources of income, concealment of......

item.title: Swechchhasebak Dal leader among 6 accused in case over rape threat
item.description: A university student in Rajshahi filed a case against six people, including a local leader of Swechchhasebak Dal, the youth front of the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party, on charges of rape threats....

item.title: Intern doctors stage protest against assault on female colleague
item.description: Intern doctors at Shaheed M Mansur Ali Medical College Hospital in Sirajganj staged a work stoppage and sit-in programme to protest the assault on an intern doctor and the intrusion of miscreants into the female intern hostel....
